title: Fear Garden — Chapter 4: The Misting Line
description: Chapter 4 of the Fear Garden saga. A staged electrical death reveals that surveillance, private footage, and digital automation have been used to control the story around the Beer Garden.

answerKey.method: Sabotaged the patio misting line and electrified a wet microphone stand
answerKey.motive: To silence Vivian before she revealed Darius sold private event footage to a rival promoter
answerKey.keyEvidence: Misting valve timer, video-sale invoice, and darius’s booth automation log
answerKey.explanation: Darius Cole had the access, timing, and motive. The proof path combines misting valve timer, video-sale invoice, and Darius’s booth automation log with the failed statement: Darius claimed the booth software was offline. The misting-line murder proves someone has been using digital systems to hide, sell, and rewrite what happened inside the garden.
answerKey.sagaConnection: The misting-line murder proves someone has been using digital systems to hide, sell, and rewrite what happened inside the garden.
answerKey.chapterRevealHook: Darius killed to silence Vivian, but the footage invoice exposes the buyer. The shell company is not just stealing money. It is preparing to take control of the Beer Garden’s future.

phone.title: Phone Evidence 1
phone.text: Vivian LeBlanc missed two calls from Darius Cole before the garden music cut out. This clue belongs to Chapter 4: The Misting Line.

phone.title: Phone Evidence 2
phone.text: A final call lasted eleven seconds as Vivian moved toward the side corridor.

messages.title: Messages Evidence 1
messages.text: Vivian texted Darius: “We talk tonight. I saw the numbers.” This clue belongs to Chapter 4: The Misting Line.

messages.title: Messages Evidence 2
messages.text: Darius replied: “Not at the bar. Meet by the garden gate.”

maps.title: Maps Evidence 1
maps.text: Vivian’s phone pinged near the patio garden path at 8:07 PM. This clue belongs to Chapter 4: The Misting Line.

maps.title: Maps Evidence 2
maps.text: Darius’s device appears nearby during the same short window.

openingNarration: Detectives, welcome back to Fear Garden at Mid City Beer Garden in Baton Rouge, Louisiana. This is Chapter 4: The Misting Line, built for Detective. Fear Garden is no longer a set of separate cases. It is one continuing story that grows darker and more elaborate with every chapter. The shell-company trail from Chapter 3 overlaps with private event footage sold outside official channels. Tonight's investigation focuses on Vivian Cross. This chapter raises the difficulty by combining physical setup, digital logs, paid invoices, and false statements. The conspiracy now has eyes, recordings, and leverage. Study every app carefully. Earlier details may return with new meaning, and the Root Ledger thread is now part of the case foundation.
revealScript: Case revealed. Chapter 4: The Misting Line is closed. The culprit was Darius Cole. The method: Sabotaged the patio misting line and electrified a wet microphone stand. The motive: To silence Vivian before she revealed Darius sold private event footage to a rival promoter. The proof: Misting valve timer, video-sale invoice, and darius’s booth automation log. Darius killed to silence Vivian, but the footage invoice exposes the buyer. The shell company is not just stealing money. It is preparing to take control of the Beer Garden’s future.

questions.prompt: Checkpoint 1: What makes Vivian’s death suspicious?

options.label: misting valve opened seconds before Vivian grabbed the mic
A normal spilled pint near the stage
A table number written backward
A guest closing a patio umbrella

questions.prompt: Checkpoint 2: Which timeline clue matters most?

options.label: The DJ restarted a playlist
Darius’s booth automation log controlled the patio cue
A guest paid with cash
A server dropped a tray outside

questions.prompt: Checkpoint 3: Which digital clue suggests planning?

options.label: A low battery warning
A misspelled hashtag
a video-sale invoice matches the rival promoter
A normal Wi-Fi reconnect

questions.prompt: Checkpoint 4: What clue points toward motive?

options.label: A birthday receipt
A lost wristband
An ordinary tab transfer
guest footage shows the mic stand moved after setup

questions.prompt: Checkpoint 5: Which statement begins to collapse?

options.label: Darius claimed the booth software was offline
Maya said the plants were watered
Janelle said the gate was busy
Darius said the speaker was loud

questions.prompt: Final 1: Who is responsible in Chapter 4: The Misting Line?

options.label: Maya Bell
Darius Cole
Trevor Knox
Janelle Price

questions.prompt: Final 2: How was the murder carried out?

options.label: Poisoned a shared punch bowl
Cut the stage lights only
Sabotaged the patio misting line and electrified a wet microphone stand
Staged a random parking-lot fall
To win a trivia prize
To protect a karaoke score

questions.prompt: Final 4: Which evidence best proves tonight’s crime and the larger pattern?

options.label: Misting valve timer, video-sale invoice, and darius’s booth automation log
A guest rumor and a napkin

A single missed call with no context

options.label: Maya said the music was loud
Darius claimed the booth software was offline
